---
title: "Appendix 2: Scenario 2 - Index Perspective (Nasdaq vs Dow Jones)"
output: html_document
date: "2025-04-21"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(lubridate)
library(glmnet)
library(randomForest)
library(zoo)
library(Metrics)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)

set.seed(423)
# Feature‐engineering function (lags + 5-day rolling): Reads an index file,
# computes today’s log‐return and tomorrow’s target and Adds lag‐1, lag‐2,
# and 5‐day rolling‐mean versions of every ratio and drops any rows
# with missing values
make_features <- function(path) {
df <- read_excel(path) %>%
rename(date = Dates, index = `Index Value`) %>%
arrange(date) %>%
# compute daily log-return and set next day as target
mutate(
log_ret = log(index/lag(index)),
target = lead(log_ret)
) %>%
# for each ratio column, add lag1, lag2, and 5-day rolling mean
mutate(across(
-c(date,index,log_ret,target),
list(lag1 =~lag(.,1),
lag2 =~lag(.,2),
roll5 =~rollapplyr(., width=5, mean, fill=NA)),
.names = "{.col}_{.fn}"
)) %>%
# remove rows where log_ret, target, or any feature is NA
filter(!is.na(log_ret), !is.na(target)) %>%
drop_na()
# extract feature matrix X, response y, and dates
list(
X = df %>% select(-date,-index,-log_ret,-target) %>% as.matrix(),
y = df$target,
dates = df$date
)
}
# generate features for Dow Jones and Nasdaq
dow <- make_features("DOWJONES 10-Year Daily Return.xlsx")
ndq <- make_features("NASDAQ 10-Year Daily Return.xlsx")
```

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# We loop over two splits (70/30 and 80/20),
# we split by time and fit OLS (baseline), LASSO (with CV), and Random Forest
# Then compute out‑of‑sample MSE, MAE, R², and correlation
train_fracs <- c(0.7, 0.8)
all_stats <- list()
for(fr in train_fracs) {
# split function (first fr% train, rest test)
split_fun <- function(obj) {
n <- nrow(obj$X)
i <- floor(n * fr)
list(
Xtr = obj$X[1:i, ], ytr = obj$y[1:i],
Xte = obj$X[(i+1):n, ], yte = obj$y[(i+1):n]
)
}
dow_sp <- split_fun(dow)
ndq_sp <- split_fun(ndq)
# Function to fit and collect metrics
fit_eval <- function(Xtr, ytr, Xte, yte) {
# OLS
df_tr <- as.data.frame(Xtr); df_tr$y <- ytr
lm_mod <- lm(y~
., data=df_tr)
p_lm <- predict(lm_mod, newdata=as.data.frame(Xte))
# LASSO
cv_l <- cv.glmnet(Xtr, ytr, alpha=1)
p_l <- predict(cv_l, s=cv_l$lambda.min, newx=Xte)
# RF
rf_mod <- randomForest(x=Xtr, y=ytr,
mtry=floor(sqrt(ncol(Xtr))),
ntree=500, maxnodes=30)
p_rf <- predict(rf_mod, newdata=Xte)
# collect metrics
stats <- tibble::tibble(
Model = c("OLS","LASSO","RandomForest"),
MSE = c(mse(yte,p_lm), mse(yte,p_l), mse(yte,p_rf)),
MAE = c(mae(yte,p_lm), mae(yte,p_l), mae(yte,p_rf)),
R2 = c(1-sum((p_lm-yte)^2)/sum((yte-mean(yte))^2),
1-sum((p_l-yte)^2)/sum((yte-mean(yte))^2),
1-sum((p_rf-yte)^2)/sum((yte-mean(yte))^2)),
Corr = c(cor(p_lm,yte), cor(p_l,yte), cor(p_rf,yte))
)
return(stats)
}
# Collect for Dow & Nasdaq
dow_stats <- fit_eval(dow_sp$Xtr, dow_sp$ytr, dow_sp$Xte, dow_sp$yte) %>%
mutate(Index="Dow Jones", Split=fr)
ndq_stats <- fit_eval(ndq_sp$Xtr, ndq_sp$ytr, ndq_sp$Xte, ndq_sp$yte) %>%
mutate(Index="Nasdaq", Split=fr)
all_stats[[as.character(fr)]] <- bind_rows(dow_stats, ndq_stats)
}
results <- bind_rows(all_stats)
# View numeric table
print(results)
```

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# MSE by Model / Split / Index
ggplot(results, aes(x=Model, y=MSE, fill=factor(Split))) +
geom_col(position="dodge") +
facet_wrap(~Index) +
labs(title="Out-of-Sample MSE by Model & Train/Split",
fill="Train/Frac", y="MSE") +
theme_minimal()
```

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
# Keep only the columns related for ΔMSE
imp <- results %>%
select(Index, Split, Model, MSE) %>%
# Pivot so that each row is one Index/Split
pivot_wider(
id_cols = c(Index, Split),
names_from= Model,
values_from = MSE
) %>%
# Compute Δ = MSE(OLS) − MSE(LASSO)
mutate(Delta = OLS- LASSO)
ggplot(imp, aes(x=factor(Split), y=Delta, color=Index, group=Index)) +
geom_line() + geom_point(size=3) +
labs(title="LASSO Improvement over OLS (ΔMSE)",
x="Train/Frac", y="MSE(OLS)-MSE(LASSO)") +
theme_minimal()
```

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Compare LASSO vs RF directly
ggplot(results %>% filter(Model!="OLS"),
aes(x=Model, y=MSE, fill=Model)) +
geom_col() +
facet_grid(Index~Split) +
labs(title="MSE: LASSO vs Random Forest",
y="MSE") +
theme_minimal()
```

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
# Bar chart of R-squared by Model / Split / Index
ggplot(results, aes(x=Model, y=R2, fill=factor(Split))) +
geom_col(position="dodge") +
facet_wrap(~Index) +
labs(
title = "Out-of-Sample R² by Model & Train/Split",
x = "Model",
y = expression(R^2),
fill = "Train/Frac"
) +
theme_minimal()
```

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Compute ΔR² = R²(LASSO) - R²(OLS)
imp_R2 <- results %>%
select(Index, Split, Model, R2) %>%
pivot_wider(
id_cols = c(Index, Split),
names_from = Model,
values_from= R2
) %>%
mutate(Delta_R2 = LASSO- OLS)
print(imp_R2)
```

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# 3) Plot ΔR²
ggplot(imp_R2, aes(x=factor(Split), y=Delta_R2, color=Index, group=Index)) +
geom_point(size=3) +
geom_line() +
labs(
title = expression("LASSO Improvement over OLS ("*Delta*R^2*")"),
x = "Train Fraction",
y = expression(R^2[LASSO]- R^2[OLS])) +
  theme_minimal()
```
